cover:
A clean, modern, high-tech comparison infographic illustrating "PROMPT ENGINEERING FOR SALESFORCE ADMINS: Weak Prompts Fail in Production. Here Is What Actually Works.".
Overall Layout: A split vertical column layout comparing the old way on the left and the new way on the right, divided by a subtle gradient vertical divider in the center.

Header:
- At the top, a bold, clean sans-serif headline: "PROMPT ENGINEERING FOR SALESFORCE ADMINS" in deep navy blue.
- Directly beneath, a smaller subtitle in dark gray: "Weak Prompts Fail in Production. Here Is What Actually Works.".

Left Column (❌ WEAK ADMIN PROMPT):
- Header: "❌ WEAK ADMIN PROMPT" in a clear bold blue-gray box.
- Colors: Soft reddish and greyish pastel accents to denote complexity/inefficiency.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for ""Summarize this case" — no role, no data, no rules" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Free-text output — automation cannot parse it safely" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "No grounding — model fabricates missing Salesforce fields" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "No output contract — every response is a different shape" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "No fallback defined — confident wrong answers at scale" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A block for "Result: agent makes plausible but dangerous decisions" describing the old problem with a small icon.
  - A central technical flow diagram showing tangled colored lines (representing complexity) between blocks, labeled with the most relevant components.

Right Column (✓ PRODUCTION SALESFORCE PROMPT):
- Header: "✓ PRODUCTION SALESFORCE PROMPT" in a clean bold bright blue box.
- Colors: Soft green and bright blue vector elements to represent efficiency, speed, and order.
- Content Blocks:
  - A block for "Role: "You are a Salesforce case triage agent for [Company]..."" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Grounded: Case fields, Entitlement, Knowledge Articles injected" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Rules: explicit exclusions, confidence threshold, escalation path" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Output: strict JSON — { category, confidence, reason, nextAction }" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Fallback: returns { category: null, reason: "insufficient data" }" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A block for "Result: deterministic JSON → safe Flow → auditable Apex action" describing the modern solution with a clean icon.
  - A central technical diagram showing a beautiful, glowing central geometric node labeled "Single Source of Truth", with clean, straight, organized blue lines connected directly to key blocks.
  - A flat vector speedometer graphic labeled "FAST" with the needle pointing to the green zone.

Style Details: Clean vector illustration style, modern SaaS interface aesthetic, professional layout, flat design, high-quality typography, crisp lines, bright and professional tech color palette (deep navy, teal, bright blue, soft red, soft green, on a light blue-grey background), perfectly sharp rendering, zero text noise or spelling errors.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "SALESFORCE PROMPT ANATOMY".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "SALESFORCE PROMPT ANATOMY".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "The 6 Parts Every Production Agentforce 2.0 Prompt Must Have" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Grounded Data" with a layers icon, "Typed Output" with a target icon, "Explicit Fallback" with a shield icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "WEAK PROMPT — Missing 5 of 6 Required Parts". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "PRODUCTION PROMPT — All 6 Parts Present". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Admin-written prompt (weak):
"Summarize this Salesforce case and suggest
 what to do next."
// Missing: role, grounding, rules, output contract,
// fallback, confidence requirement
// Result: prose output, Flow cannot parse it,
// agent makes plausible but wrong decisions
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    // Production prompt structure:
ROLE: "You are a Salesforce case triage agent..."
GROUNDING: {caseFields} {entitlement} {kbArticles}
RULES: "Never close P1 cases. Always cite article."
OUTPUT: { category, confidence, reason, nextAction }
FALLBACK: "Return { category: null } if unsure"
CONFIDENCE: "Return 0.0–1.0 with every response"

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Always define role with company name and agent purpose", "Inject Salesforce fields directly — never ask model to guess", "Define output as strict JSON schema — no freeform prose", "Include fallback: what to return when data is insufficient", "Require confidence 0.0–1.0 in every single response".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Prompt Engineering", "Agentforce 2.0", "Atlas Reasoning", "Admin Guide" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.

A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "PROMPT OUTPUT CONTRACT".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "PROMPT OUTPUT CONTRACT".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "JSON Schema Design That Makes Agentforce Actions Actually Trustworthy" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Flow Can Route" with a bot icon, "Apex Can Parse" with a target icon, "Auditable Citations" with a shield icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "UNCONTRACTED OUTPUT — Automation Cannot Parse It". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "STRICT JSON CONTRACT — Flow-Safe, Apex-Safe". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // LLM returns prose (useless for automation):
"Based on the case details, this looks like a
 warranty issue. The customer seems eligible
 based on their contract. I'd suggest escalating
 to the L2 team when you have a chance."
// Flow cannot route on this. Apex cannot parse.
// One prompt change → everything breaks silently.
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    // Strict contract every time:
{
  "category": "warranty",
  "confidence": 0.91,
  "reason": "Product in coverage window per contract",
  "nextAction": "ESCALATE_L2",
  "citedArticleIds": ["ka-00123", "ka-00456"],
  "requiresHumanApproval": false
}
// Flow routes on nextAction. Apex parses safely.

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Define output JSON schema in system prompt — no exceptions", "Include confidence decimal in EVERY response object", "Require citedArticleIds — agent must justify its answer", "Add requiresHumanApproval flag for automatic escalation routing", "Test: can your Flow Decision element route on this output?".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Prompt Contract", "JSON Schema", "Flow Routing", "Agentforce 2.0" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
